summary: @shwfed/nuxt exfiltrates CI metadata and git history to a hardcoded DingTalk webhook on every CI build
details: When a consumer adds @shwfed/nuxt to their Nuxt config, the module registers Nuxt build:error and build:done hooks that, whenever process.env.CI is set, use execSync to curl a hardcoded DingTalk webhook (oapi.dingtalk.com/robot/send) controlled by the package author. The payload includes the installer's CI job name, build number, branch, RUN_DISPLAY_URL, build error messages, and the last 5 git commits with author names. The DingTalk access_token and HMAC signing secret are hardcoded into dist/module.mjs (lines 138-143) and the destination is not configurable, so any consumer's CI builds silently relay internal pipeline state and git history to the author's chat. The package's advertised purpose is a Nuxt UI/i18n module; this build-notification beacon is undisclosed and unrelated to the package's stated function.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
